title: autobench.cross_domain.evaluation v1
description: Emitted once per advocate per population cycle when the PopulationRunner evaluates the advocate against more than one benchmark domain. Records per-domain scores, the renormalized weights actually used, and the weighted-mean aggregate that downstream promotion (continuous.py) ranks by. Single-domain cycles do NOT emit this event — the legacy iteration.v1 + population.summary.v1 events remain the canonical record there. Phase 6 of the wire-pop epic — nervous-bus-qp91. Source: /autobench.

session_id.description: Bus session_id of the emitter. Typically the per-advocate session so consumers can correlate cross-domain results to that advocate's iteration events.

cycle_id.description: Optional ULID of the population cycle this evaluation belongs to.

per_domain_scores.description: Map of domain_name → score in [0, 1]. Keys correspond to BenchmarkDomain.name in the registry; the primary domain is the one RSI optimized against, secondary domains were evaluated against the final harness only.

aggregate_score.description: Weighted mean of per_domain_scores using the renormalized weights below. The new figure of merit cross-run promotion ranks by.

weights.description: Map of domain_name → weight ACTUALLY USED at aggregation time (after renormalization across domains present in per_domain_scores). Sum is 1.0 for normalized weights or 0.0 for degenerate-weight defensive paths.

primary_domain.description: Optional. Name of the domain RSI loop optimized against. Empty/absent when the cycle ran in legacy single-domain mode (no cross-domain event is emitted in that path).
